# Copilot Instructions for Astrolabe

> **Note:** The repository currently has no source files. These instructions will be updated as the project grows.

## 1. Project Overview

Astrolabe is intended to house code related to mathematical networking utilities.
At the moment there are no modules, but future code may include:

- Core computation engines (`/src`?)
- Networking layers for distributed calculations
- CLI or web interfaces

When files appear, focus on reading multiple components to understand their interactions.

## 2. Workspace Structure

Initially the workspace is empty. When code is added, pay attention to:

- `src/` or `lib/` directories for implementation
- `tests/` for unit and integration tests
- Any build tools (Makefile, package.json, etc.)

Update these instructions with concrete paths.

## 3. Developer Workflows

Currently no build or test commands are defined. Once dependencies are added, look
for scripts in `package.json`, `Makefile`, or other config files. Document them here.

Example placeholders:

```bash
# install dependencies
npm install

# run tests
npm test
``` 

## 4. Conventions and Patterns

No conventions detectable yet. When code appears, note:

- Naming patterns (e.g. `compute_*`, `net_*`)
- Error handling style (exceptions, result objects, callbacks)
- Module export style (ESM vs CommonJS)


## 5. Integration Points

Currently none. Future instructions should call out third-party APIs, databases,
message queues, etc., with example configuration files.


## 6. AI Guidance

- Always verify the workspace contents since it may change often.
- Ask clarifying questions if theres insufficient information to implement a task.
- Prioritize adding documentation to this file as you learn the codebase.


> _Feedback welcome:_ If any section is unclear or you spot missing details, let me know
and I can expand or correct it.
